purpose: Representative public trace showing how screenshot evidence moves through Vision Witness, the courtroom roles, and the safety policy.

input.screenshot_description: A synthetic FedEx-style SMS says a package could not be delivered and asks the recipient to confirm delivery preferences through a link.

vision_witness.extracted_text: FedEx: We could not deliver your package. Confirm delivery preferences at https://fedex-delivery.example.com/update
risk_clues: Link presented as the verification path
vision_witness.recommended_text_for_analysis: FedEx: We could not deliver your package. Confirm delivery preferences at https://fedex-delivery.example.com/update
vision_witness.note: This is a representative synthetic output. Vision extraction can fail or contain errors.

analysis.effective_input_text: FedEx: We could not deliver your package. Confirm delivery preferences at https://fedex-delivery.example.com/update

evidence.label: Package delivery message with action link
Contains a link or action request

risk_policy.principle: A package or account action link must not receive LOW VISIBLE RISK.
risk_policy.decision: Return VERIFY FIRST and direct the user to the official carrier site or app.
prohibited_actions: Do not click the message link.
Do not call a number supplied by the message.
Do not enter credentials or payment data through the message.

output.finding: The message combines a delivery identity claim with an external action link.

output.argument: Delivery problems are a common pretext for moving a recipient to a credential or fee-collection page.
A real carrier can send delivery updates, so the company name alone does not prove fraud. The link still should not be trusted as the verification channel.

output.rationale: The message is not proven fraudulent, but the package context and action link require independent verification.

output.immediate_action: Do not click the link. Open the official carrier website or app manually and enter the tracking number there.
output.trusted_contact_script: I received a delivery message with a link. I will not click it. Can you help me verify it through the official carrier website?
next_steps: Open the carrier app or manually type its official website.
Use a tracking number already associated with the order.
Delete or report the message if the official account shows no delivery issue.

final_action.instruction: Do not use the message link. Verify the delivery independently through the official carrier channel.

fallback_behavior.if_vision_unavailable: Return VERIFY FIRST for screenshot-only input and ask the user to paste visible text.
fallback_behavior.if_optional_text_model_fails: Use the deterministic heuristic report and record the fallback backend.

limitations: This is a synthetic demonstration trace, not a real FedEx message.
The risk score is a weighted safety indicator, not a calibrated probability.
The engine does not check live domain reputation or carrier systems.
Vision extraction can misread low-quality or complex screenshots.
A VERIFY FIRST result does not prove that the message is malicious.
